questions.q: What is the concept of Fundamental Rights?
questions.answer: Fundamental Rights are the basic rights of citizens that are guaranteed by the Constitution of India. They are enshrined in Part III of the Constitution and include rights such as equality, freedom of speech and expression, and right to life and liberty. These rights are meant to protect the individual from the arbitrary exercise of power by the state.

Four Fundamental Rights are the Right to Equality, Freedom of Speech and Expression, Right to Life and Liberty, and Right to Freedom. These rights are essential for the protection of individual rights and freedoms.

questions.q: What is the significance of Right to Equality?
questions.answer: The Right to Equality is a fundamental right that ensures that all citizens are treated equally and without any discrimination. It prohibits any form of discrimination on the basis of caste, creed, sex, or place of birth. This right is essential for promoting social justice and equality.

questions.q: What is the concept of Right to Freedom?
questions.answer: The Right to Freedom is a fundamental right that guarantees the freedom of speech and expression, assembly, association, movement, and residence. It also prohibits the state from imposing any arbitrary restrictions on individual freedoms.

questions.q: What is the significance of Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression?
questions.answer: The Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression is essential for the functioning of a democratic society. It allows individuals to express their opinions, ideas, and thoughts without any fear of censorship or persecution.
questions.topic: Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression

questions.q: What is the concept of Right to Life and Liberty?
questions.answer: The Right to Life and Liberty is a fundamental right that guarantees the protection of life and liberty of all citizens. It prohibits the state from taking away the life or liberty of any individual without due process of law.

questions.q: What is the concept of Directive Principles of State Policy?
questions.answer: The Directive Principles of State Policy are a set of guidelines that are enshrined in Part IV of the Constitution of India. They are meant to guide the state in the formulation of policies and laws that promote social and economic welfare of the citizens.

questions.q: Name any two Fundamental Duties of a citizen.
questions.answer: Two Fundamental Duties of a citizen are to abide by the Constitution and respect the national flag and the national anthem. These duties are essential for the promotion of national integration and social harmony.
key_points: Respect the national flag and anthem

questions.q: What is the significance of Fundamental Duties?
questions.answer: Fundamental Duties are essential for promoting a sense of responsibility and citizenship among individuals. They help in building a harmonious and integrated society.

questions.q: What is the concept of Social Rights?
questions.answer: Social Rights are rights that promote the social and economic welfare of citizens. They include rights such as right to education, right to employment, and right to healthcare.
